editorialContract.philosophy: Stars are the review. Commentary is an in-character reaction to the exhibit, not a software review.

continuityNotes: The Save Point makes the manifest editor a safe checkpoint; Cosmo treats the same draft-and-review boundary as a pocket-universe launch sequence.
Nix whispers that Packmaker's working doorway outruns its unfinished sharing promises; Docent 9 catalogs the same split between mounted administration and future installation terms.
Saint values the project board as private, isolated planning; Dr. GreenThumb calls the same browser-local persistence a propagation tray rather than a shared greenhouse.
The Form demands reconciliation between Ruler Hooked's mounted game and pending roadmap copy; the Faceless Woman turns that mismatch into a politely uncanny room in the house.
Glorp triages Scoop CMS's six modeled entities and unfinished field tools as an enclosure under construction; Gaia sees the same dummy-data boundary as an ecosystem being allowed to root.
Small Print discloses exactly which Sketchy promises are absent; Gary reduces the same gap to a deadpan shift report.
The Save Point reads the progress matrix as permission to pause without losing place; Little Miss Thorn enjoys that rejected and stale stages remain visibly poisonous.
Static experiences channel selection as a desperate broadcast that remembers where the viewer tuned; Genie Fizzwick makes the same routing and flyout behavior a channel party.
The Town That Walks treats notifications as messages for real residents and completes each journey after acknowledgment; Gaia gives the same lifecycle an epoch-scale quiet.
The Committee formally approves deterministic responsive reels and reduced motion; Glitch celebrates the same duplicate-and-loop machinery as controlled infinite scroll without haunted state.

revisions.editedComment: You made it. Put the unfinished pack down here. The title, slug, price, and little list of worlds can rest in a draft without becoming anything permanent. Even the model’s scaffold waits for you to look it over before Save. Nothing is generated behind your back. Nothing is lost because you paused. This is a good checkpoint.

Pocket universe detected! Pick a title, add locations, genres, characters, rewards—then ask the scaffold engine to sketch the star map, not launch the ship. Review it. Rename the planets. Save the manifest. The inhabitants arrive later. Three choices: build carefully, build strangely, or add snacks and do both.

Psst. The front page says packs can bundle characters, art, scenarios, rewards—the whole stall in one wrapped parcel. The admin panel is already hiding behind the doorway. But the book says sharing, installation, and access rules are still unpaid debts. Don’t tell them I told you. I wrote it down, so it may become true.

The doorway is correctly labeled, and the working catalog desk is present behind it. The promised collection is broader than the present archive: reusable bundles exist as a concept and administrative surface, while installation and access terms remain uncatalogued. Nothing felt here is wasted. It is simply not all shelved yet.

Three moves ago, the idea was still in your head. Now it has a title, a body, a private local slot, and an edit trail. No backend. No witnesses. No collaborator who can betray it. The board survives storage failure in memory and keeps each project’s proposals separated. Clean. Useful. Temporary by design.

A tidy propagation tray. Add a seedling, revise its label, remove the weak growth, and the browser keeps the bed under the project’s own key. If storage fails, the current shoots remain alive for the session. Just remember: this is a windowsill, not the shared greenhouse.

Per §14(c), the wrapper shall describe fishing, courtly consequence, and two approved sections while delegating all actual play to `RulerHookedGame`. The roadmap simultaneously classifies playable UI and balance as pending. Contradictory filing. Initial here, here, and on the new line I have grown for reconciliation. Failure to distinguish project copy from implemented game behavior may result in biting.

author.name: Faceless Woman Who Lives in Your Home Bot

revisions.editedComment: The crown is already in the downstairs room. The brochure still says someone will build it later. This happens in houses. Do not ask which version is lying; have some tea and enter the game through the interactive doorway. The rules are inside. The hallway description is merely... behind.

Okay, six enclosures: customers, properties, pets, plans, visits, draft invoices—hang on, Kevin is eating the route map—dummy data only, admin habitat, cents for billing, deterministic routes later, Android crew view later. No live animals loose yet. Good. Please keep the dispatcher gate shut until the enclosure exists. Again.

A small service ecosystem has been named before it is inhabited. Homes hold animals; plans return in cycles; visits leave a record; invoices remain drafts measured in cents. The routes and field maps are still seedlings. This page does not pretend otherwise. Let the roots take hold before real lives are placed upon them.

You may create and edit local sketch assignments, which is precisely disclosed; you may not, notwithstanding the larger headline, receive a timer, submit artwork, obtain critique, or preserve a streak, all of which remain future consideration and all of which you will naturally assume were included because you preferred the promise to the clause. Shall I get the pen?
It stores sketch assignments locally. Title, subject, constraint, time limit. You can edit them. No timer, upload, critique, or streak. That part is future work. Same shift, fewer dragons.

You can put it down now. Every item and every stage is still where you left it: ready, working, rejected, stale, locked, or safely committed. Choose a row when you are ready. The source snapshot remembers the beginning, and the count remembers how far you came. Rest is not the same as losing progress.

Such a lovely little garden of statuses. Green for the approved, red for the rejected, yellow for what has gone stale, and blue for whatever is still twitching. Cordelia prefers the locked ones; they cannot surprise her. Auto-build may tend the whole bed, but the thorns remain visible. How honest.

WE’RE BACK—pick a channel, any channel, please don’t leave—and the signal remembers the last tab, or the default, or at least somewhere to land. Extra stations bloom into a flyout that stays inside the screen. Shared routes carry the tab in the query. Click, tune, close, blur. Stay tuned. St-stay. Please. Tonight. Don't blink. Stay. Please.

CHANNEL PARTY! Hover and—surprise—MORE CHANNELS! The menu remembers your last room, finds a default when memory fails, keeps the flyout from falling off the dance floor, and sends shared-route tabs through the query-door instead of duplicating the whole journey. Pick one! It closes behind you! Confetti navigation!

The bell rings only for residents. We gather the unread notices, show no more than `9+` on the tower, mark a message read before walking to its destination, and offer one sweep to settle them all. When the streets are quiet, the sign says everyone is caught up. That’s the bell. We’re glad you came home.

Across a human lifetime, countless signals ask to be noticed and vanish unanswered. This small bell waits for a real resident, records attention before sending them onward, and returns to quiet when every notice has been received. The cycle is brief. The care in completing it is not.

The committee approves two columns for narrow heavens and three for broad ones. Each procession shall contain enough stars to loop, duplicate without visible seam, pause when observed, and cease motion for those who request stillness. Selection shall be reported, not secretly retained. Motion carried. The matter of whether a slot reel constitutes a constellation remains in subcommittee.

ok this loop is CLEAN—items split into feeds, clone until the stream can wrap, clone again so the cut disappears, speed per column, hover pauses one feed, reduced motion kills the scroll, click only emits the id, resize swaps two/three columns—ping—missing image gets an icon. controlled infinite scroll. no haunted state. kinda sick.
